params.name: Euler's Scripts
params.description:
On Jun 6, 1909 AD, a large number of Euler's manuscripts were discovered in St. Petisberg, which greatly disrupted the Swesh Federation of Natural Sciences' plan to collect and publish his works.
These manuscripts come from the Swiss mathematics master Euler. As the most prolific mathematician in history, he wrote an average of more than 800 pages every year. Even though he was blind in his later years, he could continue his research with the help of clerks by virtue of his amazing memory and …
